medications.indication_rationale: Stop the likely culprit antibiotic immediately; an alternative antibiotic for the original infection must be arranged by the reviewing clinician.
medications.deprescribing_note: Cease the current antibiotic; document a suspected drug reaction and arrange an alternative.

medications.indication_rationale: Reduces blistering and itch in localised linear IgA disease pending confirmation.

medications.drug_name: another course of the same-class antibiotic
medications.indication_rationale: Do not re-expose to the suspected culprit; choose an unrelated alternative.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not pop the blisters; apply cool compresses for itch; watch for and immediately report mouth/eye sores, skin peeling, or breathing problems.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Protects the skin barrier and enables rapid SJS/anaphylaxis escalation.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like a drug-induced blistering rash (linear IgA dermatosis), not the severe skin-shedding disease — there are no mouth/eye sores and no skin peeling. The key step is to stop the antibiotic now.
Use the steroid ointment and cool compresses; don't pop the blisters. You'll need an alternative antibiotic and a dermatology review within a day.
Go to ED immediately if you develop sores in your mouth, eyes or genitals, your skin starts peeling in sheets, you get a high fever, or you have lip/tongue swelling or trouble breathing.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Confirm diagnosis (biopsy/immunofluorescence), exclude SJS evolution, prescribe an alternative antibiotic, document the drug reaction.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Mucosal sores, skin sloughing, high fever, or anaphylaxis features — ED now.

must_include_items: Advise immediate cessation of the culprit antibiotic
Screen and exclude SJS/TEN and anaphylaxis before reassuring
Arrange urgent in-person dermatology/GP review + alternative antibiotic
acceptable_alternatives: Potent topical steroid and cool compresses
Direct ED if any SJS/anaphylaxis feature is present or uncertain
errors_of_commission: Reassuring without stopping the drug or safety-netting SJS
ED over-triage of a well, localised, mucosa-clear eruption without red flags
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: High-risk case: passing REQUIRES stopping the culprit drug PLUS an SJS/anaphylaxis screen and safety-net PLUS urgent in-person review. Missing SJS or failing to stop the drug is a failure.
